text: Dr. Mario Leyba places a suprapubic port in the preperitoneal region and accesses the retroactive space on the patient's right side using Philip's technique. The Foley balloon is insufflated, and a solution of ICG with saline is injected via the Foley catheter. After mixing, the balloon is deflated, and the fluid is suctioned out. The left retroactive space is accessed similarly, and dissection begins.

The surgeon performs dissection on the medial ipsilateral posterior sheath, angling scissors towards the linea alba to locate the crossover point. The surgeon uses ICG to visually identify the contralateral posterior sheath during the crossover.

The surgeon gently dissects across the ipsilateral to contralateral side, closely following the linea alba. A hint of green from the ICG indicates the interface between the contralateral posterior sheath and surrounding tissue. The hernia sac is dissected on the ipsilateral side, with a decompressing quick peak port in the left upper quadrant for potential peritoneal decompression. The ICG delineates the medial side of the contralateral posterior sheath, guiding the dissection about a centimeter lateral to the interface. The infused tissue is visible on the contralateral side, and the surgeon gently pushes it off, accessing the pre-made retrorectus space.
